title: A Prompt Is Not a Lock
summary: Real boundaries must sit outside the model.
centralClaim: Probabilistic instructions cannot enforce deterministic authority boundaries.

title: Flexibility Hides Runtime Cost
summary: Visible limits make misuse easier to contain.
centralClaim: Flexibility is useful only when the layer can safely absorb the cost of ambiguity.

title: The Instruction Worked Too Well
summary: Authority turns vague intent into real damage.
centralClaim: A system can satisfy the written instruction while violating the real intent.

title: Prevention Will Fail Somewhere
summary: Design the blast radius before compromise arrives.
centralClaim: A useful security posture defines what happens after one control fails.

title: Savings Can Move Risk
summary: Lower cost without hiding new failure.
centralClaim: Optimization is only useful when it reduces cost without quietly moving risk.

title: Find What Cannot Change
summary: The fixed constraint makes the problem smaller.
centralClaim: The fixed constraint usually makes an unfamiliar problem smaller.

title: The Bottleneck Was Smaller
summary: Measure before buying complexity you must operate.
centralClaim: Operational complexity should follow evidence about the bottleneck, not precede it.

title: Willpower Was Not the System
summary: Change the conditions that keep choosing for you.
centralClaim: Better choices often come from changing conditions, not demanding more discipline.

title: Failures Worth Writing Down
summary: A backlog for turning concern into inspectable proof.
centralClaim: Reliability writing should turn concern into named failures, violated invariants, and inspectable containment.
